title: Workspace-Aware Infrastructure Configuration
description: Write a Terraform configuration that uses terraform.workspace to select instance types and creates workspace-tagged resources.
starterCode:
# Write a Terraform configuration that:
# 1. Uses a local 'env_config' map with workspace-specific settings:
#    default -> instance_type=t3.micro, instance_count=1
#    staging -> instance_type=t3.small, instance_count=2
#    production -> instance_type=t3.large, instance_count=5
# 2. Creates aws_instance with count from env_config
#    and instance_type from env_config using terraform.workspace
# 3. Tags each instance with Environment = terraform.workspace
# 4. Outputs current_workspace showing terraform.workspace value

# Locals block with workspace config map

# EC2 instances with count and type from workspace config

# Output
hints: terraform.workspace returns the name of the current workspace
count.index is 0-based within a counted resource
Always have a 'default' key in your map for the default workspace

validationRules.errorMessage: Use terraform.workspace to select workspace-specific config
Define an env_config map in locals
Include a 'production' entry in the workspace config map
Set the instance count from workspace config

simulatedOutput.success:
Current workspace: staging

Terraform will perform the following actions:
  + aws_instance.app[0] will be created (t3.small)
  + aws_instance.app[1] will be created (t3.small)

Plan: 2 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.

Outputs:
  current_workspace = "staging"
simulatedOutput.failure: Workspace configuration error. Check terraform.workspace usage, env_config map, count, and output block.
